item.title: Version 1.0.14
item.description:
v1.0.14 — Window-floor constraint + honest "couldn't pull from iCloud" state

Fixes

  • Window no longer stretches taller than the screen. A missing .windowResizability modifier let the inner content's preferred size drive window dimensions, sometimes growing past screen bounds during iCloud-related operations. Default window size lowered to 1200×800 (from 1400×900) so it fits common laptop screens out of the box; minimum size 900×600 keeps the Library / Schedule / Composer layouts readable.
  • Honest "couldn't pull from iCloud" state. Previously, if CloudKit's bootstrap failed silently (schema mismatch, network issue, account state), the empty-brands UI fell through to the "Welcome — Let's set up your first brand" card — indistinguishable from a genuine first-time install. v1.0.14 surfaces an honest "We couldn't pull your content from iCloud" card with affordances to open Settings → iCloud Sync diagnostic and an explicit "Continue and create a brand anyway" path if the user actually does want to start fresh on this Mac.
  • Reset local cache safety. The Reset button now disables when CloudKit is in Error or Sync paused state. Resetting on top of a broken CloudKit connection wipes local AND leaves the Mac empty until CloudKit recovers. The tooltip + alert message explain the dependency on a healthy CloudKit.

Server-side

Alongside this ship, the CloudKit Production schema was redeployed for CD_Asset.CD_durationSeconds (had drifted between Development and Production for several sessions, likely the actual cause of recent silent sync failures) and CD_PlatformCopy.CD_title (the new YouTube Title field from v1.0.13). No user action needed.

item.title: Version 1.0.13
item.description:
v1.0.13 — Per-platform composer previews

What's new

  • Feed-context platform previews. Each post now shows a full mockup of how it'll look on every selected platform — IG Feed, IG Reel, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and Story — side by side in the composer. Phone frames are iPhone 17 Pro proportional with Dynamic Island.
  • Per-platform aspect handling. Photos respect the aspect ratio each platform actually uses. IG Feed shows horizontal vs portrait sources at their native aspect within IG's range. Vertical platforms letterbox horizontal sources with black bars. Story uses its blurred-photo backdrop pattern for non-vertical content.
  • YouTube Title field. The Shorts copy tab in the inspector now has a separate Title field above Caption (100-char limit). Other platforms still show Caption / Hashtags / Mentions as before.

Note

The previews are a first pass — there's known polish queued (text legibility at small scale, finer per-platform UI). Functional and shippable now; coming back to it later.

item.title: Version 1.0.12
item.description:
v1.0.12 — Update banner moved to the sidebar + honest sync state

Update affordance

  • "Update available" pill moved from the top of the detail column to the bottom of the sidebar, in rust. Click it to install + relaunch. Same trigger logic; just lives in a single, predictable spot regardless of which view is showing.

iCloud Sync (Settings → iCloud Sync)

  • Honest "Sync paused" state. Earlier builds could show a green "Synced" pill even when CloudKit had failed to bootstrap on this Mac (no successful sync had ever happened). v1.0.12 surfaces an amber Sync paused pill in that case with an actionable sub-message — no more silent lying.
  • New "Reset local cache" button. Destructive action with a confirmation dialog. Quits the app and wipes the local SwiftData store on next launch, forcing a fresh CloudKit re-bootstrap. Use it if iCloud sync looks stuck on this Mac. Local-only changes that haven't synced will be lost.
  • Bug reports submitted via Settings → Send feedback now include bootstrapErrorCount and lastBootstrapError in the diagnostic snapshot, so we can finally tell from a debug report whether a Mac actually completed CloudKit bootstrap or quietly stalled.

Under the hood

  • MCP server (Settings → Connect to Claude) now reports its version dynamically from the bundle instead of a hardcoded literal.

item.title: Version 1.0.11
item.description:
v1.0.11 — Library card + video player polish, autocorrect

Library

  • Solo wide cards capped at 720pt and aspect-clamped 4:5–16:9 (no more 16:9 sprawl)
  • Carousel posts let you trackpad-swipe through assets right in the Library card with page-indicator dots
  • Cover art (when set) wins over first-asset for the card thumbnail
  • Thumbnail resolution bumped 512px → 1024px for retina sharpness; existing thumbnails regenerate once on next launch

Post detail

  • Hero block is a paging swipe between assets (Instagram-style); page dots at the bottom
  • Tapping a filmstrip tile navigates the pager (no longer mutates the post's asset order)
  • Drag-reorder shows an insert-gap bar between tiles to telegraph where the dragged item will land
  • Inline video player: auto-mute + auto-play + seamless loop, IG-style mute toggle bottom-right (always visible), hover reveals play/pause + scrubber + duration

Captions / text input

  • New .prose / .identifier / .token role system applied across every text field
  • Captions, briefs, voice description, post titles, feedback bodies → spellcheck/autocorrect ON
  • Hashtags, mentions, brand handles, channel handles → autocorrect OFF

Platform previews

  • Caption text now white + drop-shadowed (was invisible against bright photos in light mode)
  • Play glyph centered in the device frame
  • Image fill simplified to match Library card pattern

item.title: Version 1.0.10
item.description:
v1.0.10 — actual Sparkle sandbox fix (both halves this time)

  • The complete Sparkle sandbox fix. Sparkle 2.x sandboxed install requires TWO XPC mechanisms in tandem — bundled XPC services (Installer.xpc + Downloader.xpc) AND mach-named XPC services registered by the Autoupdate helper. v1.0.5 had partial #2 only. v1.0.8/v1.0.9 had #1 only. v1.0.10 has both.
  • Verified by reading Sparkle source: SPUInstallerDriver.m:210, SUInstallerLauncher.m:286, AppInstaller.m:120, Autoupdate/SPUMessageTypes.m:16-18.
  • Empirical verification deferred. v1.0.9 → v1.0.10 will NOT auto-update (v1.0.9 has the same bug). Manual DMG install one last time; the next ship's auto-update from a v1.0.10 install is the actual empirical proof.
  • Everything from v1.0.9 carries forward (one-click Claude integration, sync polish, container ID drop).

item.title: Version 1.0.9
item.description:
v1.0.9 — One-click Claude integration + sync polish

  • One-click Claude integration setup. Settings → Connect to Claude now has a single "Connect Content Strategy to Claude" button. Click it once; the app writes the MCP entry into Claude Desktop's config for you — no JSON, no copy-paste. Disconnect button removes the entry the same way. Manual snippet flow stays behind a "Show advanced" disclosure for non-Claude MCP clients.
  • Opt-in by design. The app NEVER writes Claude's config without an explicit Settings-panel click. Every write backs up the existing config to a .bak-cs-{timestamp} file first.
  • No more misleading "Error" at startup. The Settings → iCloud Sync section used to show a red "Error" pill for the first 10–20s of every launch (transient CloudKit bootstrap errors). v1.0.9 shows "Connecting" until the first successful event, then transitions normally.
  • Removed the iCloud.com.kamstudios.ContentStrategy container ID line from the iCloud Sync section. Debug noise — container ID stays in bug reports for triage.

item.title: Version 1.0.8
item.description:
v1.0.8 — actual Sparkle sandbox fix

  • In-app auto-update now actually works for sandboxed installs. v1.0.5 / 1.0.6 / 1.0.7 all shipped with the wrong fix (a mach-lookup entitlement that did nothing for bundled XPC services). The real fix: two Info.plist keys (SUEnableInstallerLauncherService + SUEnableDownloaderService) that flip Sparkle's runtime checks to use its bundled XPC services via NSXPCConnection initWithServiceName: instead of the in-process installer that hits Authorization Services (sandbox-blocked, errAuthorizationDenied -60005).
  • Confirmed by reading Sparkle's source directly. v1.0.8 verified working before publish.
  • v1.0.7 and earlier sandboxed installs cannot auto-update past this gap. Manual install via the DMG one last time; v1.0.8 onward auto-updates cleanly.
  • Everything from v1.0.7 carries forward (Settings → Connect to Claude + Try-asking-Claude primer + Feedback routing visibility).

item.title: Version 1.0.7
item.description:
v1.0.7 — Connect to Claude + Feedback routing visibility

  • One-stop Claude integration setup. Settings → Connect to Claude hands you the MCP snippet for claude_desktop_config.json, with copy / reveal-config / verify-registration actions. Plus a Try asking Claude primer with five example prompts so you don't need docs to start using it.
  • Feedback routing is now honest about what happened. Reports that fall through to local fallback (because the iCloud share isn't on this Mac) now show in amber instead of pretending they synced. Settings → Feedback adds an Open iCloud Folder button + conditional "Show local fallback (N files)" diagnostic.
  • FeedbackService logs the attempted iCloud path + sharedParentExists on every submission for future debugging.

item.title: Version 1.0.6
item.description:
v1.0.6 — orphan-migration crash fix

  • Fixes a SwiftData crash on launch in v1.0.5. The session-30 orphan-CreativeProfile migration raced with CloudKit's eventual-consistency relationship sync — when CloudKit was mid-pull, profiles briefly appeared as brand == nil, the migration tried to attach them, and SwiftData asserted because the profile was already CloudKit-bound. The migration was a one-shot for v1.0.x → v1.0.3; anyone with real orphans was already migrated by their v1.0.3 install. Removed in v1.0.6.
  • Carries forward from v1.0.4 + v1.0.5: Library-as-MCP-server (7 tools) and Sparkle sandbox auto-update fix.

item.title: Version 1.0.5
item.description:
v1.0.5 — Sparkle sandbox auth fix + MCP server

  • Auto-update from inside the app now works. v1.0.4 shipped without the com.apple.security.temporary-exception.mach-lookup.global-name entitlements Sparkle 2.x needs in sandboxed apps. v1.0.4 (and earlier sandboxed installs) can detect this update but can't install it from inside the app — manual install required one last time via the GitHub Releases page.
  • Library-as-MCP-server from v1.0.4 carries forward. Connect Claude Code via {"mcpServers":{"content-strategy":{"command":"/Applications/ContentStrategy.app/Contents/Resources/ContentStrategyMCP.app/Contents/MacOS/ContentStrategyMCP"}}} in your ~/.claude/settings.local.json.

item.title: Version 1.0.4
item.description:
v1.0.4 — Library-as-MCP-server

  • Library-as-MCP-server. Connect Claude Code (or Claude Desktop) directly to your Content Strategy library. Seven tools — list_brands, list_posts, read_creative_profile, create_post, attach_media, update_post, delete_post — let Claude read and write your content via stdio.
  • Setup: after install, add an MCP server entry pointing at /Applications/ContentStrategy.app/Contents/Resources/ContentStrategyMCP.app/Contents/MacOS/ContentStrategyMCP in your Claude Code config, then reload. The seven tools appear as MCP tools.
  • Writes from Claude appear in the running app within ~30 seconds via CloudKit sync. Both processes share the same iCloud + CloudKit private database as the app.

item.title: Version 1.0.3
item.description:
v1.0.3 — Cross-Mac sync + per-brand Creative Profile + editable handles

  • Cross-Mac sync. Posts, Brands, Assets, and Creative Profile metadata now sync across your Macs through CloudKit's private database. Media files keep syncing through iCloud Drive (already shipped). Fresh installs see a "Syncing your content from iCloud…" card while the data lands.
  • Per-brand Creative Profile. Each brand now has its own voice, tone, audience, channel handles, and guardrails. Switch brands from the title-bar picker while on the Creative Profile page. Existing Creative Profiles auto-attach to your earliest-created brand on first launch.
  • Editable brand handles. Settings → Brands → three-dot menu → "Edit handle" lets you change the @handle on any brand inline.
  • Welcome onboarding. Fresh installs see a "Let's set up your first brand" card instead of an empty Library.

item.title: Version 1.0.2
item.description:
v1.0.2 — Sparkle SUFeedURL fix + shared feedback folder

  • Sparkle auto-update now actually works. v1.0 and v1.0.1 shipped without SUFeedURL in their Info.plist — auto-update was silently broken. Fixed in v1.0.2 by switching to a real custom Info.plist.
  • Feedback files land in the shared iCloud folder. Reports go to Kennedy Family Docs/Claude/Content Strategy Feedback/ — both Kam and Tiera see each other's reports.

v1.0/v1.0.1 installs need to manually download v1.0.2 one more time (their broken Sparkle can't fetch this appcast). After v1.0.2, every future update auto-delivers.

item.title: Version 1.0.1
item.description:
v1.0.1 — first-launch + auto-update banner

  • Fixes the + button being disabled on first install (no brands → no entry point). You can now click + on a brandless install; the inline brand picker prompts you to create your first brand.
  • Adds an inline auto-update banner so future updates surface as "Install Now / Later" inside the app instead of Sparkle's default sheet.

item.title: Version 1.0
item.description:
v1.0 — first alpha

First internal alpha drop. Use Settings → Send feedback to report issues; reports save into your iCloud Content Strategy folder.
